questions.question: A right triangle has an angle of 35° and the side opposite to it is 12 cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Use sin(35°) = 12/h, so h = 12/sin(35°) ≈ 12/0.574 ≈ 20.9 cm. This is a direct SOH-CAH-TOA application.

questions.question: If cos(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, find sin(θ).
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean identity: sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1. So sin²(θ) = 1 − (3/5)² = 1 − 9/25 = 16/25, giving sin(θ) = 4/5 (positive in Q1).

questions.question: A ladder leaning against a wall makes a 65° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how high up the wall does it reach? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 65° angle. Use sin(65°) = h/10, so h = 10 × sin(65°) ≈ 10 × 0.906 ≈ 9.1 feet.

questions.question: On the unit circle, a point has coordinates (−1/2, √3/2). What angle in degrees (between 0° and 360°) corresponds to this point?
questions.explanation: The point (−1/2, √3/2) is in the second quadrant with cosine = −1/2 and sine = √3/2. This matches the 60° reference angle, so the angle is 180° − 60° = 120°.

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(x) + sin²(x)·cot²(x)
questions.explanation: Factor: sin²(x)(1 + cot²(x)). Since 1 + cot²(x) = csc²(x) = 1/sin²(x), we get sin²(x) · 1/sin²(x) = 1.

questions.question: From the top of a 50-meter cliff, the angle of depression to a boat is 28°. How far is the boat from the base of the cliff? Round to the nearest meter.
questions.explanation: The angle of depression equals the angle in the right triangle. Use tan(28°) = 50/d, so d = 50/tan(28°) ≈ 50/0.532 ≈ 94 meters.
